text.title: templates Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for templates. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: CLAUDE.md is a single line: `@AGENTS.md` (the `@` symbol tells Claude Code to load and inline the target file). No other content.
rule.rationale: Claude Code only auto-loads CLAUDE.md; the @-ref hook loads AGENTS.md automatically. This is the single mechanic that makes one file (AGENTS.md) serve both Claude Code and standalone agents.

rule.statement: AGENTS.md is between 20–80 lines (max ~5k tokens). It answers: what is this directory, what commands run here, what are the gotchas, what are the key paths, where are deep references.
rule.rationale: Below 20 lines, AGENTS.md cannot answer "how do I work in this directory"; above ~80 lines (5k tokens), the routing brief itself becomes context pollution for the agent. Empirically, 20-80 lines covers what agents need to know at entry.

rule.statement: .agents/INDEX.md lists deep references (architecture, API, decisions, migrations, troubleshooting) as a table. Agents load this only when specifically asked or when a topic is not covered in AGENTS.md.
rule.rationale: Architecture deep-dives, decision records, and migration guides stay searchable but off the critical path — agents only load them if needed, keeping the hot-path context minimal.

rule.statement: CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md are required in every directory that contains source code or agent-relevant logic, including subpackages, modules, test dirs, and CLI tool folders. Not just the repo root.
rule.rationale: A monorepo with src/, test/, scripts/, tools/ requires each subdir to have its own pair so an agent opening src/auth/handlers.py finds auth-specific context without loading repo-root generalities or test-dir irrelevancies.

rule.statement: CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md may be skipped only in: empty __init__.py-only directories with no logic, vendored dependencies, generated build output, and trivial leaf folders with less than 3 files and no documented gotchas.
rule.rationale: The per-directory pair adds overhead; skip it only when the overhead clearly exceeds benefit (a generated folder that gets rewritten, an empty __init__.py marker, or a three-line utility no agent should touch).

rule.statement: AGENTS.md must include, in order: (1) one-paragraph summary, (2) Commands section with at least test and lint, (3) State section with phase and health, (4) Key Paths table, (5) Gotchas section with concrete traps, (6) Related section linking to .agents/INDEX.md and parent brief.
rule.rationale: This six-section structure ensures AGENTS.md answers what/where/how/when/why for agent onboarding. Missing sections leave agents without critical information (e.g., no Commands = agent doesn't know how to test; no Gotchas = agent learns the hard way).

rule.statement: .agents/INDEX.md is a table with two columns: path (directory or filename) and description (one-line). It lists architecture/, api/, decisions/, migrations, troubleshooting, and any other deep reference that AGENTS.md doesn't cover in full.
rule.rationale: Agents often need architectural context or decision history, but those documents are long and should not bloat AGENTS.md. The INDEX table is a pointer — agents load the specific doc only if needed.

rule.statement: Monorepo root AGENTS.md answers "What is this repo?" and lists subpackages with one-line descriptions. Subpackage AGENTS.md answers "What is this package, how do I test and deploy it here?" Subdir AGENTS.md may inherit from parent if logic is identical, but different-logic subdirs require their own pair.
rule.rationale: An agent opening src/auth/ needs auth-specific test commands and auth-specific gotchas; it should not have to read the repo root brief to find "cd src/auth && npm test". Per-dir pairs save context tokens and reduce onboarding time.
